# Understanding Our World from Space

> *A hands-on workshop for prospective students at the University of Liverpool*

[<img src="assets/gds_logo_1.png" alt="Imago Logo"
width="300"/>](https://liverpool.ac.uk/geographic-data-science)
[<img src="assets/Imago-logo.png" alt="Imago Logo"
width="300"/>](https://imago.ac.uk)

Welcome! This page contains everything you need to follow **Understanding Our World from Space**, a workshop for Applicant Discovery Day at the University of Liverpool, delivered by the [Geographic Data Science Lab (GDSL)](https://www.liverpool.ac.uk/geographic-data-science/) and the [Imago Data Service for Imagery](https://imago.ac.uk/).

Every day, satellites orbiting Earth capture vast amounts of imagery — from bustling cities to remote coastlines. But how do we turn these pictures into knowledge? How can a computer "understand" what's in a satellite image, and use that understanding to answer real-world questions?

In this workshop, you'll get a taste of what it's like to study geographic data science at Liverpool. No coding experience is required — everything runs in your browser.

## Workshop Details

**Audience:** Prospective undergraduate students at Applicant Discovery Day\
**Location:** University of Liverpool

## What you'll do

The session is in **two parts**:

1. **From Pixels to Patterns** — an interactive walk-through of how researchers turn raw satellite imagery of London into a map of what's on the ground. You'll see the full pipeline — from individual bands of light, to composite images, to a machine-learning classifier that labels every pixel as water, park, building, or cloud.
2. **Interactive exploration of embeddings** — you'll use a live web tool built by the Imago team to search the UK by *what places look like from space*. Pick a neighbourhood and instantly see the ten most similar places across the country, powered by a cutting-edge AI technique called **satellite embeddings**.

Together, these two parts take you from the foundations of satellite analysis to the frontier of what the field is doing right now.

## Schedule

| Time | Session |
|---|---|
| Part 1 | **From Pixels to Patterns** — reading London from space |
| Part 2 | **Interactive exploration of embeddings** — finding lookalike places across the UK |

See the [Materials](materials.qmd) page for the links you'll need during the session.

## Who is this for?

Applicants considering degrees in **Geography, Environmental Science, Data Science**, or related subjects at the University of Liverpool. No coding background is required.

## Requirements

None

::: {.callout-tip}
## Interested in going further?

If you enjoy today's session, come and study with us. Liverpool offers undergraduate degrees in **Geography**, **Environmental Science**, and **Data Science** — all of which open doors into geographic data science research of the kind you've seen today.

Have a chat with the academics running the workshop, or explore:

- [Geographic Data Science Lab (GDSL)](https://gdsl-ul.github.io/)
- [Imago Data Service for Imagery](https://imago.ac.uk/)
:::

## Colophon

This is an open resource hosted on [Github](https://github.com/Imago-SDRUK/ADD_from_space). If you want, you are welcome to peek at the [repository](https://github.com/Imago-SDRUK/ADD_from_space), its [license](LICENSE), or even [contribute](CONTRIBUTING.md)!
